meta.name: Finance / Crypto Dashboard Design System
meta.style: Modern Soft UI – Card-based fintech dashboard
meta.aesthetic: Light neomorphic with bold accent cards, muted sage background, high-contrast data readouts
meta.use_case: Financial data dashboards, crypto portfolio trackers, analytics UIs
design_principles: Cards as primary layout unit — each metric lives in its own elevated tile
High contrast between card types (dark, white, color-fill) for visual hierarchy
Minimal chrome — no heavy borders or dividers, whitespace does the separation work
Data is the hero — numbers are large, labels are small and muted
Accent color signals interactivity and positive states

color.description: Full palette used across the UI

background.page_note: Muted sage green — the global canvas. Every card floats on this.
background.card_dark_note: Near-black, used for hero stats / primary feature card
background.card_green_note: Bright mint green — high-energy accent card (balance, BTC price)
background.card_purple_note: Soft violet — secondary accent card (market cap, charts)

text.secondary_note: Muted gray for subtitles, timestamps, labels
text.positive_indicator_note: Bright green used for upward trend badges and delta labels

accent.primary_green_hex_note: Main accent — fills cards, progress bars, icon backgrounds
accent.dot_indicator_note: Small purple dot used as a live/active position marker on gauges

border.shadow_note: Very soft drop shadow on white cards to lift them off the page bg

chart.bar_primary_note: Tall bars in bar charts — bright green
chart.bar_secondary_note: Contrasting bar segments within the same chart
chart.gauge_filled_note: Dark arc on the balance gauge card

font_stack.note: Modern geometric sans. Clean, neutral, highly legible at all sizes. Data-first.

label.usage: Subtitles, timestamps, axis labels ('Updated 1 days ago', 'Sell currency')

radius.card_note: All dashboard cards use a large, consistent border radius
radius.button_note: Pills and action buttons are fully rounded
radius.inner_card_note: Nested elements / inner tiles within cards (e.g., BTC price sub-card)

icon.style: Rounded stroke icons, weight ~1.5px stroke. Monochrome within context.

motion.usage: Hover lift on cards, badge count updates, chart bar entry

card_base.description: The universal wrapper for every dashboard tile

metric_display.description: Large number + optional label. The hero element of any data card.

suffix.note: Currency symbol or unit appended inline, same size or slightly smaller

badge_pill.description: Small inline label — used for filter state (Monthly), delta % (+28.21%)

icon_button.description: Circle or rounded square button with a single icon. Navigation, actions.

avatar.description: User avatar — circular, with optional overlap (stacked avatars)
avatar.note: Two avatars overlap using negative margin-left

transaction_row.description: Single row in a transaction list. Icon + title/subtitle on left, value on right.
transaction_row.divider: none — row separation by padding only

bar_chart.description: Grouped vertical bars. Simple, no axes shown, labels above columns.
bar_chart.notes: Bars are stacked or grouped in pairs. No grid lines. Labels only on groups.

gauge_chart.description: Semicircular arc gauge. Used for balance / score visualization.

position_dot.note: Marks the current value position on the arc

area_chart.description: Smooth area chart — used for market cap sparkline inside purple card.

progress_bar.description: Horizontal progress indicator. Used for BTC price percentage target.

timeline.description: Vertical timeline. Year + description pairs. Bullet dots indicate milestones.

sub_card.description: Smaller nested card within a parent card. Used for BTC price and market cap.

green_sub_card.header: flex row, card label + external link icon (top-right arrow)

grid.column_distribution: roughly 50/50 or 40/60 depending on card content weight
grid.pattern: 2×2 macro grid with internal sub-grids inside right column

large_card.example: Sales statistics (dark card), Current balance (green card)

wide_card.example: Market forecast (white card spanning full right column)

internal_card_layout.header_row: title left, action/badge right — always flex space-between
internal_card_layout.data_row: metric value large + label small stacked vertically
internal_card_layout.chart_placement: bottom or right of card, never overlapping text
internal_card_layout.avatar_row: flex row, avatars overlap at top-right of card header

right_column_layout.description: The right half of the grid is subdivided into 3 zones

zones.content: Recent Transactions card — white, 1 row tall
Market Forecast white card — spans left portion + 2 sub-cards stacked on right

zones.layout: vertical stack of 2 color-fill cards (green BTC price, purple market cap)

data_card_anatomy.description: Reusable pattern for any metric tile

color_card_hierarchy.description: Card color communicates data priority and category
rules: Dark card = primary KPI / main stat — highest visual weight
Green card = positive / growth / balance — energy and action
Purple card = forecast / secondary value — softer, speculative
White card = structured data / lists / timelines — neutral container

number_formatting.currency_note: Symbol follows number, not precedes — stylistic choice
number_formatting.percentages: Sign-prefixed with decimal ('+28,21%'), comma as decimal separator

icon_usage.action_icons: External link arrow — top-right of sub-cards, signals drill-down
icon_usage.navigation_icons: Left/right chevrons in pairs for pagination / time navigation
icon_usage.status_icons: Small colored dot or arrow for trend direction

interactive_states.hover_card: Slight lift — transform: translateY(-2px), shadow deepens
interactive_states.selected_period: Badge background fills to accent green

css_variables.description: Map all tokens to CSS custom properties at :root

replication_checklist: Set global background to sage (#D8E5DB) — this is load-bearing to the aesthetic
All cards must have 24px border radius — no exceptions
Numbers use tabular-nums and negative letter-spacing for density
Charts are decorative and low-detail — no axes, labels only where essential
Never mix card color variants in a single column — alternate dark/white or color/white
Sub-cards inside a white card use the color variants (green/purple) — not more white
Avatar groups use negative margin overlap, 2px white border ring
Spacing inside cards is generous — do not compress padding below 16px
